datasources.description: Phase or plat identifier (numeric, e.g., 123). Combines with Project and LotNo. to form the 3-tuple join key required to avoid Harmony-style lot collisions (~$6.75M risk). Phase fill is 100% in Lot Data, unlike GL feeds.
datasources.embedding_text:
Phase — Collateral Dec2025 01 Claude.xlsx - Lot Data — int
Phase or plat identifier (numeric, e.g., 123). Combines with Project and LotNo. to form the 3-tuple join key required to avoid Harmony-style lot collisions (~$6.75M risk). Phase fill is 100% in Lot Data, unlike GL feeds.
samples: '123', '456', '8', '9', '10'

datasources.description: Unnamed: 5: GAAP-basis collateral valuation (at cost). Numeric string with trailing spaces; values range from ~$3.4M to $64.5M. Represents accounting book value of the asset pool.
datasources.embedding_text:
Unnamed: 5 — Collateral Dec2025 01 Claude.xlsx - RBA-TNW — string
Unnamed: 5: GAAP-basis collateral valuation (at cost). Numeric string with trailing spaces; values range from ~$3.4M to $64.5M. Represents accounting book value of the asset pool.
samples: 'GAAP at Cost', '3,428,845.0', '64,537,008.0', '2,423,581.9', '50,016,594.4'

datasources.description: Unnamed: 6: Market-value collateral valuation (at appraised/market value). Numeric string with trailing spaces; values range from ~$3.8M to $89.2M. Typically higher than GAAP cost for appreciated inventory.
datasources.embedding_text:
Unnamed: 6 — Collateral Dec2025 01 Claude.xlsx - RBA-TNW — string
Unnamed: 6: Market-value collateral valuation (at appraised/market value). Numeric string with trailing spaces; values range from ~$3.8M to $89.2M. Typically higher than GAAP cost for appreciated inventory.
samples: 'At Value', '3,844,000.0', '89,196,805.0', '4,278,000.0', '50,214,783.8'

datasources.description: Unnamed: 7: Lender advance rate assumption per asset bucket (e.g., '90%', '80%'). Percentage string used to calculate potential borrowing base from collateral value.
datasources.embedding_text:
Unnamed: 7 — Collateral Dec2025 01 Claude.xlsx - RBA-TNW — string
Unnamed: 7: Lender advance rate assumption per asset bucket (e.g., '90%', '80%'). Percentage string used to calculate potential borrowing base from collateral value.
samples: 'Assumed Adv Rate', '90%', '80%', '75%', '70%'

datasources.description: Unnamed: 8: Potential Borrowing Base: the maximum lendable amount for each collateral bucket after applying the assumed advance rate. Computed as 'At Value' × 'Assumed Adv Rate'. Numeric string; ranges from ~$3.5M to $71.4M.
datasources.embedding_text:
Unnamed: 8 — Collateral Dec2025 01 Claude.xlsx - RBA-TNW — string
Unnamed: 8: Potential Borrowing Base: the maximum lendable amount for each collateral bucket after applying the assumed advance rate. Computed as 'At Value' × 'Assumed Adv Rate'. Numeric string; ranges from ~$3.5M to $71.4M.
samples: 'Potential Borrowing Base', '3,459,600.0', '71,357,444.0', '3,208,500.0', '35,150,348.7'

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Allocation Engine
datasources.description: Community: Canonical project/community name (e.g., Arrowhead Springs). Primary key dimension for the allocation model. Corresponds to `canonical_project` in operating state v2.1.
datasources.embedding_text:
Community — Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Allocation Engine — string
Community: Canonical project/community name (e.g., Arrowhead Springs). Primary key dimension for the allocation model. Corresponds to `canonical_project` in operating state v2.1.
samples: 'Arrowhead Springs', 'Ben Lomond', 'Harmony', 'Lewis Estates', 'Parkway Fields'

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Allocation Engine
datasources.description: Phase: Numeric phase identifier within the community (e.g., 10, 11, 12). Combined with Community forms the phase-grain allocation key. Typically corresponds to `canonical_phase` after normalization.
datasources.embedding_text:
Phase — Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Allocation Engine — int
Phase: Numeric phase identifier within the community (e.g., 10, 11, 12). Combined with Community forms the phase-grain allocation key. Typically corresponds to `canonical_phase` after normalization.
samples: '10', '11', '12', '13', '14'

datasources.table_name: Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Allocation Engine
datasources.description: Job Id: Optional external job/cost-center reference (float, nullable). Typically unpopulated in v3 snapshot; used for cross-reference to upstream accounting systems if needed.
datasources.embedding_text:
Job Id — Flagship Allocation Workbook v3.xlsx - Allocation Engine — float

datasources.field_name: Flagship Homes — Budget Allocation Workbook v2
sample_values: One row per phase per lot type (SFR, TH, MF, Condo).
LotData counts pre-populated. Enter manual lot counts (col F) and avg projected sales price (col H).
Tie-out (col G) = LotData minus Manual. Goal is zero.
Total Projected Sales (col I) = Lots x Price — this drives the allocation basis.
datasources.description: Flagship Homes — Budget Allocation Workbook v2: Multi-line instruction text describing the structure and usage of the Flagship Homes Budget Allocation Workbook v3. datasources.description: In Collateral Pool?: Yes/No flag indicating whether this phase is pledged to the lender. Determines if phase appears in borrowing-base calculations; all rows in this export carry Yes.

sample_values: Found 2 columns (AA and AJ) with SUMIFS referencing '[Collateral Dec2025.xlsx]Vert'!$F/$A/$B. Replaced all with OfferMaster!$F/$A/$B. Both columns do the same lookup: match Community (col A) and Product Type (col B) to return Total cost (col F). 38 formulas updated per column (76 total). Values unchanged — e.g., Harmony SFR=268K, TH=197K, MF=185K matching OfferMaster.
Reviewed Claude Log and found Turn 4 had completed updating 76 SUMIFS formulas from external Vert references to local OfferMaster. No pending tasks.

Confirmed Avg Cost per Home (col AA/AJ) values are identical — formula change is validated. Found 339 total differences, most due to period-over-period data movement (homes progressing through pipeline). Flagged 6 items warranting review: HARMONY B1, SALEM FIELDS A TH, MEADOW CREEK 6, PF B2, PF C2, PF G1. Also noted 4 new rows, 6 Lot Type changes, and total lender advance up $13.9M (+9.4%).

Net +90 lots driven by: +190 new lots from 4 new Harmony phases (B2 71, B3 47, ADB13 34, ADB14 38) and lots appearing in Meadow Creek 6/8, Scarlet Ridge 1, PF A1. Offset by -100 lots leaving other rows (Harmony B1 -60, Salem Fields A TH -81, PF G1 -62, PF D2 -16, Willowcreek 1 -20, Willowcreek 2 -8).

User edits: (1) Offsites Allocation reduced ~34.5% across Salem Fields rows, (2) ITD Dev Costs updated across ~15 rows, (3) Remaining Dev Costs significantly reduced across multiple PF/Salem/Scarlet/WC rows, (4) MC6 lots set to 0, (5) # Closed increased for MC6=68, MC8=48, PFA1=2. No negative values or Advance>Value issues found. Total lots now 1,266 (-28 vs Prior). Lender Advance +$16M (+10.8%). Offsites down $2.3M, ITD Dev up $11.4M, Remaining Dev down $16.3M.
